to: Linux Claude Code (Guided AI Labs website repo: guided-ai-labs-website)
status: FYI — no action required on the website side
summary: The Guided AI Labs intake Microsoft Form was updated (intent/path question reworded + a new CRM column). NOTHING on the website needs to change: the form URL is unchanged and the CTA is just a link, so there is no mismatch. This is an awareness note only, mirroring what was sent to the Journey repo.

whatChanged.formQuestion: The optional choice question is now 'What best describes your situation?' with four richer choices (Just me / My team / My organization / Governance or policy, each with a descriptive tail).
whatChanged.newCrmColumn: Answers now map into a dedicated CRM Choice column 'IntentPath' (display 'Intent / Path') on CRM - New Signals, in addition to the SourceText provenance footer.
whatChanged.formUrlUnchanged: The Labs form URL did NOT change (in-place edit, same question id). CTA still resolves correctly.

whyNoMismatch: The website only holds the CTA link to the form. The form -> flow -> CRM field/column mapping is entirely tenant-side and is already updated and verified (Chunk-3 verifier 0/0; end-to-end test for the Labs brand ALL CHECKS PASS; test records cleaned up, 0 residue). The site never references the form's internal questions or columns.

headsUpNextInitiative.title: Custom-built intake form to replace Microsoft Forms (planned)
headsUpNextInitiative.note: Adam has decided to move from the boring Microsoft Forms shell to a CUSTOM intake form built into the site, styled to match each brand's landing page, posting to an HTTP-triggered Power Automate flow into the SAME CRM. A full integration spec will follow as a separate packet. The current Microsoft Forms path stays LIVE as the fallback until the custom path is built and end-to-end verified — do not remove the existing CTA yet.

_staging.status: STAGED — release to X:\ when ready.
_staging.releaseInstruction: Copy to X:\WINDOWS_TO_LABS__crm-intake-intent-live.json and replace this _staging block with a _meta block marked RELEASED.
